meta.reportLabel: Tue flash-PMI day — US-Japan FX coordination signal, oil 3-mo low / into PCE
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40
meta.regime: Dollar-strong / yen-weak. Warsh's hawkish FOMC stuck — DXY fresh 13-mo high ~101 (intraday 101.13), dots flip next move to a HIKE. USD/JPY ~161.5 (near 1986 low); intervention risk now coordination-flavoured after the Katayama-Bessent call.
meta.nextBigEvent: US PCE — Thu Jun 25 (the marquee test of Warsh's 'broader than energy' call)

dailyRead: Three deltas Tuesday, all reinforcing the dollar-strong / yen-weak core rather than breaking it. (1) The yen story escalated from words to coordination signalling: FinMin Katayama spoke by phone with US Treasury Secretary Bessent and reaffirmed an agreement to coordinate FX action 'if needed' — a step up from Monday's verbal-only posture. USD/JPY ~161.5 (near the 1986 low), the yen has surrendered all its Apr 30 record-intervention gains, and the explicit US-Japan coordination line raises the odds the next move is JOINT, not unilateral — historically a harder, faster snap that hits every XXX/JPY at once. (2) The dollar pushed to a fresh 13-month high (~101, intraday 101.13) on the intact Warsh regime — the long-USD trade is even more crowded into Thursday's PCE. (3) Oil slid again: Brent ~$77.5, WTI ~$74 (near the early-March low) after Washington's 60-day Iranian oil license and 30M+ barrels shipped through Hormuz last week — but the peace track snagged: Switzerland confirmed the Friday Burgenstock technical talks are cancelled, with Iran reportedly demanding Israel halt the re-erupted Lebanon fighting. So oil keeps falling on flows while the diplomatic tail re-widens a notch. Today's live release is the flash PMIs (DE, EZ, UK, US), the EUR/GBP local catalyst. Net: carry the dollar-strong / yen-weak lean, but the JPY book's intervention risk just got two-sided and louder (coordination-flavoured), and the dollar is stretched into PCE — defend, don't chase.

strength.drivers: Core PCE firm; CPI 4.2% (May); claims 229k. Fed 3.50-3.75%, hawkish hold, dots flip next move to a HIKE.
strength.forward: 🦅 Regime intact: median '26 dot ~3.8%, 9/18 see a hike. DXY fresh 13-mo high ~101. Next test: PCE Thu — a soft print is the only near-term fade. Even more crowded long.

strength.forward: 🦅 Hawkish dissent (Pill, Greene) + 'CPI may rise later' firmed GBP against a bid dollar. Flash PMI today the local catalyst; a sub-50 services print is the soft spot. Next BoE Aug.

strength.drivers: Trimmed mean ~3.9%; unemp 4.5%. RBA 4.35%, hawkish hold, minutes: 'inflation risks to the upside'.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 RBA floor + risk-on beta vs a bid dollar. AUD CPI Wed + jobs Thu are the local catalysts. Next RBA Aug.

strength.forward: 🦅↔ Destination unchanged, spot squeezed. Katayama-Bessent call signals US-Japan COORDINATION readiness → intervention risk now two-sided and joint-flavoured, the swing. Japan CPI Thu. Next BoJ late Jul.

strength.forward: ⚖️↘ Pure USD-leg: hawkish Fed pushes EUR/USD down, dovish ECB frame caps bounces. Flash PMI today (May composite 47.5, sub-50) the local catalyst. Next ECB late Jul.

USD/JPY ~161.5; Katayama-Bessent reaffirmed coordinated-action readiness. Biggest near-term swing for the whole JPY book, now joint-flavoured.
Clock ticking, but Friday's Burgenstock technical round is CANCELLED (Iran tying it to a Lebanon ceasefire). The residual oil-respike tail re-widens a notch.
NZD's next live event; hike case fading on oil.

geopolitics: Flows still beat diplomacy on price. Washington's 60-day Iranian oil license plus 30M+ barrels shipped through Hormuz last week pushed Brent to ~$77.5 and WTI to ~$74 (near the early-March low). But the diplomatic track stalled: Switzerland confirmed Friday's Burgenstock technical talks are cancelled, with Iran reportedly demanding Israel halt the re-erupted Lebanon fighting and VP Vance no longer travelling. The 60-day MOU clock keeps running, so the base case (truce holds, oil drifts lower) is intact — but the first technical round slipping plus the Lebanon flare nudges the oil-respike tail back open a notch. Transmission: oil → energy/headline inflation → CB hawkishness → FX. Base case keeps the dollar bid, supports GER40 and risk-beta AUD/NZD, and does NOT buy a Fed cut (Warsh marked inflation up into the oil drop); for CAD the broadening CPI partly offsets the renewed oil drag. Tail (re-widened a notch): an MOU stall / Lebanon escalation re-spikes oil (rescues CAD, hurts AUD/NZD/GER40, hands USD & JPY competing haven bids) — the persistent ~$4,195 gold bid is the thin premium for exactly this.
